per_query_results.nl_query: How many customers do we have?
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS customer_count FROM customers

per_query_results.nl_query: Show all products
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT * FROM products

per_query_results.nl_query: How many orders are there in total?
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders

per_query_results.nl_query: List all campaigns
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT * FROM campaigns

per_query_results.nl_query: How many support tickets exist?
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS ticket_count FROM support_tickets

per_query_results.nl_query: Show all product categories
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT category FROM products

per_query_results.nl_query: What are the different order statuses?
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT status FROM orders

per_query_results.nl_query: How many products do we sell?
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS product_count FROM products

per_query_results.nl_query: Show customers in the Enterprise segment
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT * FROM customers WHERE segment = 'Enterprise'

per_query_results.nl_query: What was our total revenue last quarter?
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT SUM(total_amount) AS total_revenue FROM orders WHERE order_date >= DATEADD(QUARTER, -1, DATEADD(QUARTER, DATEDIFF(QUARTER, 0, GETUTCDATE()), 0)) AND order_date < DATEADD(QUARTER, DATEDIFF(QUARTER, 0, GETUTCDATE()), 0)

per_query_results.nl_query: How many orders were placed in 2024?
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders WHERE order_date >= '2024-01-01' AND order_date < '2025-01-01'

per_query_results.nl_query: Show high priority support tickets that are still open
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT * FROM support_tickets WHERE priority = 'high' AND status = 'open'

per_query_results.nl_query: What is the average order value by channel?
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT channel, AVG(total_amount) AS avg_order_value FROM orders GROUP BY channel

per_query_results.nl_query: How many customers are in each region?
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT region, COUNT(*) AS customer_count FROM customers GROUP BY region

per_query_results.nl_query: Show campaigns with a budget over 50000
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT * FROM campaigns WHERE budget > 50000

per_query_results.nl_query: What are the total sales by order status?
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT status, SUM(total_amount) AS total_sales FROM orders GROUP BY status

per_query_results.nl_query: List products priced above 100 dollars
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT * FROM products WHERE list_price > 100

per_query_results.nl_query: Show top 10 customers by total spend
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT TOP 10 c.id, c.name, SUM(o.total_amount) AS total_spend FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.id, c.name ORDER BY total_spend DESC

per_query_results.nl_query: Which products have been ordered more than 100 times?
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT p.id, p.name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_ordered FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON p.id = oi.product_id GROUP BY p.id, p.name HAVING SUM(oi.quantity) > 100

per_query_results.nl_query: Show the total revenue per campaign
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT c.id, c.name, SUM(o.total_amount) AS campaign_revenue FROM campaigns c JOIN campaign_conversions cc ON c.id = cc.campaign_id JOIN orders o ON cc.order_id = o.id GROUP BY c.id, c.name

per_query_results.nl_query: List customers who have open support tickets
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT c.id, c.name, c.email FROM customers c JOIN support_tickets st ON c.id = st.customer_id WHERE st.status = 'open'

per_query_results.nl_query: What is the average order value per customer segment?
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT c.segment, AVG(o.total_amount) AS avg_order_value FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.segment

per_query_results.nl_query: Show the most popular product categories by number of orders
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT p.category, COUNT(DISTINCT oi.order_id) AS order_count FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON p.id = oi.product_id GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY order_count DESC

per_query_results.nl_query: Which customers have never placed an order?
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT c.id, c.name, c.email FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id WHERE o.id IS NULL

per_query_results.nl_query: Show customers who placed orders but also filed support tickets
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT c.id, c.name FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN support_tickets st ON c.id = st.customer_id

per_query_results.nl_query: What is the month-over-month revenue growth?
per_query_results.expected_sql: WITH monthly_revenue AS (SELECT FORMAT(order_date, 'yyyy-MM') AS month, SUM(total_amount) AS revenue FROM orders GROUP BY FORMAT(order_date, 'yyyy-MM')) SELECT month, revenue, LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS prev_month_revenue, CASE WHEN LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month) IS NOT NULL THEN ((revenue - LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month)) / LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month)) * 100 ELSE NULL END AS growth_pct FROM monthly_revenue ORDER BY month

per_query_results.nl_query: Rank customers by their total spend within each segment
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT c.name, c.segment, SUM(o.total_amount) AS total_spend, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY c.segment ORDER BY SUM(o.total_amount) DESC) AS spend_rank FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.name, c.segment

per_query_results.nl_query: What is the running total of revenue by day?
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT CAST(order_date AS DATE) AS order_day, SUM(total_amount) AS daily_revenue, SUM(SUM(total_amount)) OVER (ORDER BY CAST(order_date AS DATE)) AS running_total FROM orders GROUP BY CAST(order_date AS DATE) ORDER BY order_day

per_query_results.nl_query: Show the campaign ROI calculated as revenue divided by spend
per_query_results.expected_sql: WITH campaign_revenue AS (SELECT c.id, c.name, c.spend, SUM(o.total_amount) AS total_revenue FROM campaigns c JOIN campaign_conversions cc ON c.id = cc.campaign_id JOIN orders o ON cc.order_id = o.id GROUP BY c.id, c.name, c.spend) SELECT id, name, spend, total_revenue, CASE WHEN spend > 0 THEN (total_revenue - spend) / spend ELSE NULL END AS roi FROM campaign_revenue ORDER BY roi DESC

per_query_results.nl_query: What is the median resolution time for support tickets by category?
per_query_results.expected_sql: SELECT category, PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY DATEDIFF(HOUR, created_at, resolved_at)) OVER (PARTITION BY category) AS median_resolution_hours FROM support_tickets WHERE resolved_at IS NOT NULL

per_query_results.nl_query: Show the top 3 products per category by revenue
per_query_results.expected_sql: WITH product_revenue AS (SELECT p.id, p.name, p.category, SUM(oi.line_total) AS total_revenue, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY p.category ORDER BY SUM(oi.line_total) DESC) AS rn FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON p.id = oi.product_id GROUP BY p.id, p.name, p.category) SELECT id, name, category, total_revenue FROM product_revenue WHERE rn <= 3 ORDER BY category, total_revenue DESC

per_query_results.nl_query: Calculate customer lifetime value as total spend divided by months since first order
per_query_results.expected_sql: WITH customer_metrics AS (SELECT c.id, c.name, SUM(o.total_amount) AS total_spend, MIN(o.order_date) AS first_order, DATEDIFF(MONTH, MIN(o.order_date), GETUTCDATE()) AS months_active FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.id, c.name) SELECT id, name, total_spend, months_active, CASE WHEN months_active > 0 THEN total_spend / months_active ELSE total_spend END AS monthly_clv FROM customer_metrics ORDER BY monthly_clv DESC

per_query_results.nl_query: Compare this quarter's revenue to the same quarter last year
per_query_results.expected_sql: WITH quarterly AS (SELECT YEAR(order_date) AS yr, DATEPART(QUARTER, order_date) AS qtr, SUM(total_amount) AS revenue FROM orders GROUP BY YEAR(order_date), DATEPART(QUARTER, order_date)) SELECT q1.yr, q1.qtr, q1.revenue AS current_revenue, q2.revenue AS prior_year_revenue, CASE WHEN q2.revenue > 0 THEN ((q1.revenue - q2.revenue) / q2.revenue) * 100 ELSE NULL END AS yoy_growth_pct FROM quarterly q1 LEFT JOIN quarterly q2 ON q1.qtr = q2.qtr AND q1.yr = q2.yr + 1 ORDER BY q1.yr DESC, q1.qtr DESC

per_query_results.nl_query: What is the conversion rate for each campaign?
per_query_results.expected_sql: WITH campaign_stats AS (SELECT c.id, c.name, COUNT(DISTINCT cc.customer_id) AS converted_customers FROM campaigns c LEFT JOIN campaign_conversions cc ON c.id = cc.campaign_id GROUP BY c.id, c.name) SELECT id, name, converted_customers FROM campaign_stats ORDER BY converted_customers DESC
